feat: store villain range as specific combos instead of hand keys (#42) (#45)

Change the solver's internal range type from dict[str, float] (169 hand keys)
to dict[tuple[Card,Card], float] (up to 1326 combos). This enables suit-sensitive
equity calculations on textured boards.

Backend:
- Add _expand_range_to_combos() to convert hand-key ranges to combo-keyed ranges
- get_starting_range() now returns combo-keyed dicts
- Simplify compute_hand_equity_vs_range() and filter_range_by_equity() to iterate
  combos directly instead of expanding hand keys at each use site
- Add _aggregate_to_hand_keys() for debug output aggregation
- Add actionCounts per hand key showing combo-level action distribution
- Update mock solver to include actionCounts

Frontend:
- Add actionCounts to SolverHandResult type
- Add gradientStops prop to GridCell for dynamic N-stop CSS gradients
- Update solver HandGrid to compute gradient stops from actionCounts,
  showing per-combo action distribution with colour-coded stops

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: correct test weaknesses found in review

- sellAnalysis: remove * 0.95 from peak-median test — peak stochastically
  dominates final distribution so p50(peak) >= p50(final) is mathematically
  guaranteed for every sample; multiplier was unnecessary and too weak
- calculations: replace zero-gain broker fee test with +20% stock delta scenario
  that actually exercises Belka reduction: diff = 16.2 PLN (< 20 PLN fee),
  saving 3.8 PLN in tax — old test passed even with zero tax in both cases
- inflationProjection: tighten convergence bounds
  blendedInflationRate(10%, 120mo) = 3.5794% → assert < 4.0 (was < 5.0)
  blendedSavingsRate(8%, 120mo) = 3.9721% → assert < 4.5 (was < 6.0)
- calculations: add exact coupon bond value test — 12 monthly coupons at 5%
  annual with zero reinvestment: expected 416.2 PLN = 400 + 400*5%*81%;
  previous test only had relative comparisons (end > principal, end < cap)

151 tests passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: sync schedule fixed panel with mode-driven active state

Replace conditional rendering of schedule parameter inputs with a
fixed two-column panel. All four parameter inputs (N, Hour, Min, Day)
and the warning lamp are always present in the layout; the selected
mode drives active/inactive state via `disabled` attribute and opacity.

Active inputs get full brightness and warm white label colour. Inactive
inputs drop to 0.35 opacity via the existing `input:disabled` rule.
The warning lamp (VU Red) illuminates only when Every N Seconds is
selected; its label text is always visible at full opacity.

**Decisions documented here:**
- Value preservation: all parameter values are preserved when switching
  modes. Inactive inputs are excluded from form submission via
  `disabled` + explicit nullification in `handleSaveProcessingConfig`.
- HOUR/MIN are shared by Daily and Weekly; switching between them
  preserves the entered time values.
- N max values: Every N Hours → 23 (within a day), Every N Minutes and
  Every N Seconds → 59 (matching prior limits and keeping the shared N
  input sensible across both ranges).

Also:
- `RadioGroup` gains optional `testIdPrefix` prop for per-option
  `data-testid` generation.
- Tests updated: conditional-render checks replaced by enabled/disabled
  assertions; two new journey tests cover full mode-switching behaviour
  and inactive-input form exclusion.
- `UI_DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md` Section 12 added: Persistent State Panels /
  Mode-Driven Parameter Panels.

* feat: split-flap slots, indicator lamps, mode reorder

Replace all DashboardLamp save indicators with SplitFlapSlot — a
fixed-size panel with a hatched idle texture that flips in a message
on state change (rotateX, 100ms each half-flip; opacity crossfade
fallback under prefers-reduced-motion). Save messages auto-reset to
idle after 5 seconds via useEffect in ConfigView.

The production warning in the sync schedule panel also uses
SplitFlapSlot (color="red"), flipping in when Every N Seconds is
selected and flipping back out when any other mode is chosen. No
auto-reset — it persists until the triggering condition clears.

Sync schedule mode order updated to: Weekly, Daily, Hourly,
Every N Hours, Every N Minutes, Every N Seconds. "Every hour" renamed
to "Hourly" for grammatical consistency with Daily and Weekly. Weekly
is now the default selected mode on first render (useState default
updated to 'WEEKLY', scheduleHour default updated to 9).

Each parameter input now has a small indicator lamp (8px, Phosphor
Amber when active, unlit #2A2A28 when inactive) positioned
immediately to its left. Lamps are aria-hidden="true" — the disabled
attribute on each input already communicates availability to screen
readers; the lamps are a visual-only shortcut for sighted users.

**Decisions documented here:**
- Save slot auto-reset timeout: 5 seconds
- Default WEEKLY parameters: Hour=09:00, Min=:00, Day=MON
- Indicator lamp ARIA: aria-hidden="true" — screen readers rely on
  the disabled attribute; adding aria-label on purely decorative
  state-mirroring elements adds noise without benefit

Also updates App.test.tsx and ConfigView.test.tsx to reflect the
renamed Configuration heading, new slot testids, and mode rename.
Two new journey tests added: split-flap idle/flip/reset flow, and
indicator lamp active-state coverage.

UI_DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md Section 5 updated with split-flap message
slot pattern.

The underlying state variables are unchanged; the empty display is

fix: checkbox in items view too small

Item checkboxes were the same size (16px) as category header checkboxes.
The request is to make item checkboxes approximately double the size
while keeping row height unchanged.

### Changes

- **`CategorySection.tsx`** — item row `AppCheckbox` and `MultiCheckbox`
now use `size={28}`; category header checkboxes remain at `size={16}`
- **`MultiCheckbox.tsx`** — added optional `size` prop (default `16`);
wrapper and partial-check indicator dimensions are now computed from
`size` via named constants instead of static styles
  ```tsx
  const INDICATOR_WIDTH_RATIO = 0.6;
  const INDICATOR_LEFT_RATIO = 0.2;
  const INDICATOR_TOP_OFFSET = 1;
  ```
- **`styles.ts`** — removed `itemCheckboxIndicator` (now inline in
`MultiCheckbox`; was also a duplicate of `categoryCheckboxIndicator`)

### Row height

Row height is determined by the menu button (⋮), not the checkbox. At
28px, the checkbox stays below that ceiling, so layout is unaffected.

commit.message:
fix: drag handle should not be visible for template list items (#8)

Templates are always sorted to the top and are unique per space —
reordering them via drag-and-drop is meaningless. The drag handle was
being rendered unconditionally for all list items.

## Change

- `ListCard`: conditionally render the drag handle only for non-template
items

```tsx
// Before
{wrap(<DragHandle />)}

// After
{!isTemplate && wrap(<DragHandle />)}
```

Since dragging is only initiated via long-press on the handle, this also
implicitly prevents templates from being dragged.

fix: excessive left space on category checkbox (#10)

The category header checkbox had 16px of space to its left (between the
accent bar and the checkbox) while the space above/below was only 4px,
creating a visually unbalanced layout.

## Change

- `categoryHeader` gap reduced from `spacing.md` (16px) → `spacing.xs`
(4px), matching the `marginBlock` value already used for vertical
spacing

```ts
categoryHeader: {
  flexDirection: "row",
  alignItems: "center",
  gap: spacing.xs,   // was spacing.md
  minHeight: spacing.lg,
  marginLeft: spacing.xs,
  marginBlock: spacing.xs,
},
```

The `categoryTitle` uses `flex: 1` so it absorbs remaining space — no
other layout adjustments needed.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: punxaphil <26582898+punxaphil@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: space permissions not strict enough

Spaces lacked role enforcement — any member could invite, remove users,
or delete a space, and owners could leave their own space.

## Permission changes

- **Owner only**: Invite user, Remove user, Delete space
- **Non-owner only**: Leave space (owners cannot leave)
- Personal space: unchanged (invite shown, no leave/delete)

## Owner visibility

- Active space name row shows an "Owner" badge
- Switch-space list appends `· Owner` to spaces the current user owns
(both iOS sheet and Android action menu)

## Implementation

`isOwner` computed once in `useSpaceManagement`:
```ts
const isOwner = activeSpace?.ownerId === getUserId();
```

Propagated to `SpaceActions`, `UserList` (`canRemove` prop),
`SpaceNameRow`, and `buildLabel`. Applied consistently in both
`SpaceSheet` (iOS) and `SpaceManagementScreen`.

fix: hideTitleForThumbnailIcons has no effect (#779) `hideTitleForThumbnailIcons: true` stopped working because `renderMediaGridCard` always fell back to rendering a title when `titleContent` was `undefined` — which is exactly what `favorites-icons.ts` passed to suppress it. ## Changes - **`renderMediaGridCard` (`utils.ts`)**: Changed `titleContent` type from `TemplateResult | undefined` to `TemplateResult | typeof nothing | undefined`, and replaced `??` with an explicit `!== undefined` guard so `nothing` can suppress rendering without triggering the default fallback: ```ts // Before — undefined triggers fallback, title always renders ${titleContent ?? html`
${item.title}
`} // After — nothing passes through as an explicit "render nothing" ${titleContent !== undefined ? titleContent : html`
${item.title}
`} ``` - **`favorites-icons.ts`**: Pass `nothing` instead of `undefined` when `showTitle` is false, so the intent to suppress the title is preserved through `renderMediaGridCard`. --------- Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: punxaphil <26582898+punxaphil@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ADO PR mapping: use lastMergeCommit nested object instead of flat mergeCommitId (#150)

The Azure DevOps GitPullRequest API returns lastMergeCommit as a nested
GitCommitRef object (with commitId), not as a flat mergeCommitId string.
The previous code deserialized a non-existent mergeCommitId field, which
always returned null from real API calls, breaking commit-to-PR mapping
for all merge strategies (including squash merges).

- Add AzureDevOpsGitCommitRef record to model the nested commit reference
- Change AzureDevOpsPullRequest to deserialize LastMergeCommit (nested object)
- Add convenience MergeCommitId property to minimize downstream changes
- Update mock handler to emit lastMergeCommit: { commitId: ... } format

chore(ci): add .gitleaks.toml to allowlist false positive in commit ebb1b70

commit ebb1b7068c73a27f45e576a77ad5b5b3d7a93a64 used curl -s -u "$SONAR_TOKEN:"
which triggers the curl-auth-user rule. It is an env var reference, not a
hardcoded secret. The pattern was fixed in the following commit (631a3f1)
to use -H "Authorization: Bearer $SONAR_TOKEN" instead.
